intro: Heteranthera seubertiana, also known as Brazilian water lily or Heteranthera, is a perennial aquatic plant native to the Americas. This plant is valued for its health benefits, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as having anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Heteranthera seubertiana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Heteranthera seubertiana is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including fever and skin conditions.
